title: Glasgow traffic: 'Heavy congestion' on M77, M74 and the M8 as drivers face rush hour delays

sections.heading: Traffic delays
sections.content: Traffic Scotland warned of heavy congestion on several of Glasgow's major roads on Wednesday morning as disruption continued across the city. The M77 was carrying heavier northbound traffic between junction five near Newton Mearns and junction two, with delays of about 20 minutes and drivers advised to leave extra time.

sections.heading: Wider disruption
sections.content: Congestion was also reported on the M74 between junction three near Carmyle and M8 junction 26, where delays of around 30 minutes were expected. The disruption came as Glasgow Central station remained closed after the city centre fire, with low level services due to resume but all high level trains cancelled for the rest of the week.

sections.heading: Closures and advice
sections.content: Road closures remained in place around the fire site, including parts of Mitchell Street, West Nile Street, Gordon Street and Union Street. Superintendent Jackie Dunbar said police continued to support partner agencies, urged people to plan journeys carefully and avoid the area if possible, and said businesses outside the outer cordon could operate as normal.
